items.title: Dyadic Decisions About Effort: How Caregivers Shape Young Children’s Persistence
items.author: Julia A. Leonard, Reut ShachnaiDepartment of Psychology, Yale University
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. Persistence is essential for learning, but children cannot and should not persist at everything. How do young children decide what is worth their effort? We build a theory of young children’s state persistence as the outcome of a socially guided decision-...</article>

items.title: Toward Complementary Intelligence: Integrating Cognitive and Machine AI
items.author: Cleotilde Gonzalez, Tailia MalloyDepartment of Social and Decision Sciences, Carnegie Mellon University
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. This article calls for complementary human-AI intelligence. Rather than redefining intelligence to fit machine capabilities, we argue for designing AI that complements and extends human cognition. We distinguish betweencognitive AI, which is grounded in ...</article>

items.title: Historical Change in Midlife Development From a Cross-National Perspective
items.author: Frank J. Infurna, Yesenia Cruz-Carrillo, Nutifafa E. Y. Dey, Markus Wettstein, Margie E. Lachman, Denis Gerstorf
items.content: <article>Current Directions in Psychological Science, Ahead of Print. We summarize empirical evidence documenting that (a) U.S. middle-aged adults have displayed historical trends of elevations in loneliness and depressive symptoms and declining memory and physical health and (b) this pattern is largely confined to the ...</article>
